ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly names Nail. Vision shows a muscular green-skinned Namekian in white gi with Namekian cranial ridges lunging forward — precisely matching Nail's canonical appearance as Guru's warrior bodyguard in a white fighting garment. The Bond 2 mechanic (triggers when 2+ Battle Cards are in play) mirrors Nail's lore function as a squad fighter protecting Guru's retinue.

trivia_bullets.text: Nail served as Grand Elder Guru's personal bodyguard and was the strongest Namekian warrior on Planet Namek during the Frieza Saga. After being mortally wounded stalling Frieza, he proposed merging with Piccolo to boost Piccolo's power. The Namekian assimilation is permanent: Piccolo absorbed Nail's energy, knowledge, and fighting skill — giving Piccolo the power to challenge Frieza's second form and force the villain into his third transformation. [WebSearch: Dragon Ball Wiki — Nail / Piccolo fusion]

BT4-053 is a Rare Green Battle card with 10,000 power. Its Auto Bond 2 skill draws 1 card and grants +5000 power per attack when 2 or more Battle Cards are in play — the official BT4 strategy page specifically singles this card out and advises playing a 1-cost Namekian on turn 1 to activate Bond immediately. [dbs-cardgame.com: Series 4 Green Strategy Page]

BT4's Green color was officially described by the design team as 'mostly focused on Namekians and Lord Slug Saga.' The consecutive block BT4-052 through BT4-057 (Guru, Nail, Kami, Dende, Popo, Saonel) represents a rare instance of an entire Namekian support hierarchy — from patriarch elder down to warrior and aide — appearing in a single numbered run within one set. [dbs-cardgame.com: Series 4 Green Strategy Page]
